Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: JAZb-MYC2a/b interaction is directly assayed in Y3H.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: JAZb is implicated in JA-responsive specialized metabolism through the mechanistic assays and downstream metabolite changes.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Protein-protein repression relieved by degradation/interference is exactly what the Results assays address.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: JAZb behavior is connected to SL status via SMXL6/7 accumulation and JA signaling logic.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The paper proposes JAZb as a condition-dependent controller linking SL repressors to JA-regulated anthocyanin/phenolamide accumulation.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: The paper infers MYC2 release leading to downstream activation including MYB8/DH29, but direct JAZb regulation of those target genes is not directly measured.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: JAZb-MYC2 complex and interaction with SMXL6/7 are directly investigated.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Interactions with SMXL6/7 and MYC2a/b are directly assayed.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Y2H, Co-IP, in vitro degradation, and Y3H were all used.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper states JAZb was highly coexpressed with stem-abundant SMXL7 from a tissue atlas.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methionine titration in Y3H and SL-deficient backgrounds are explicit contexts.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly explains JAZb was selected due to coexpression with SMXL7 and interaction support.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is speculative and not tested in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper shows that boosting this JA arm does not restore nicotine-based resistance under elevated auxin conditions.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S was used for transient MYC-JAZb expression, and pBridge Met-regulated system was used in Y3H.

Omics_Data.reason: A published RNA-seq coexpression dataset was used and no accession is given here.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Anthocyanins and phenolamides are described as defense/stress-related metabolites.

Applied_Species.reason: MYC2a/b are studied in the N. attenuata system.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Nicotiana attenuata is the species used.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The experimental background is the 31st generation inbred line.

Source_Species.reason: The MYC2a/b used in Y3H are from the N. attenuata system.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: They are NaMYC2 proteins in this study context.

Regulator_Type.reason: MYC2 is described as a critical transcription factor promoting JA responses.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper does not explicitly state MYC2a/b are bHLH transcription factors in the provided text.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Anthocyanin and phenolamides are clearly linked through the model; nicotine is not directly shown as MYC2a/b-regulated in this paper’s Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Flavonoid and phenolamide are supported more directly than alkaloid/nicotine for MYC2a/b in this paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The implicated metabolites occur in stems/pith and roots as described, though MYC2 itself was not spatially profiled.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The Results infer MYC2a/b are released when JAZb repression is weakened by SMXL6/7, driving downstream JA output.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Y3H interference is direct, and the model connecting released MYC2 to downstream JA signaling including MYB8 is explicitly proposed.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: MYB8 and downstream genes are proposed, but direct targeting by MYC2a/b is not tested here.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: MYC2 is explicitly framed as a central JA-response transcription factor affecting specialized metabolism outputs.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Transcriptional activation when released from JAZ repression is directly consistent with the paper's model and Y3H logic.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: JAZ repressors, SL status via SMXL-JAZ interactions, and JA signaling are all explicit upstream inputs in the paper.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The paper mechanistically positions MYC2 as the core JA-output activator linking JAZ destabilization to metabolite accumulation.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Direct activation of specific downstream genes by MYC2a/b is not measured in this paper.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: JAZb-MYC2 interaction is directly assayed in Y3H.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Direct interaction with JAZb is shown; SMXL6/7 act by interference context, but direct MYC2-SMXL binding is not shown.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Y3H was used directly, and the broader role is inferred from pathway/genetic/metabolite data.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methionine titration in Y3H is explicit.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed does not genetically manipulate MYC2a/b and supports their role mainly mechanistically.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an extrapolated application not directly tested.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper shows broad JA activation does not ensure improved defense because auxin suppresses nicotine.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Anthocyanins/phenolamides are defense/stress-associated and nicotine affects herbivore performance, as stated in the paper.

corrections.reason: The paper does not explicitly state MYC2a/b are bHLH transcription factors in the provided text.
